name: Texas Size Pawn Shop
description_short: Family-owned Houston pawn shop offering collateral-based loans and resale of tools, electronics, jewelry, firearms, and musical instruments with over 30 years in business.
description_long: Big City Pawn is a family-owned pawn shop located in east Houston, Texas at 1300 Wayside at Navigation. The business has been operating for over 30 years and positions itself as a community-focused alternative lender and secondhand retailer. The shop accepts collateral-based loans on personal items and maintains inventory of used merchandise across multiple categories. According to their website, the business emphasizes customer respect and dignity in all transactions. The company operates a physical storefront and invites walk-in customers to browse merchandise and negotiate deals. Big City Pawn also offers cash for gold and jewelry items, providing liquidity options for customers looking to sell personal valuables. The business maintains basic online presence with contact information and merchandise categories listed on their website. As a traditional pawn operation, the company functions as both a lender and a secondhand goods retailer, allowing customers to either pawn items temporarily or sell them outright. The 30-year operating history suggests established relationships with the local Houston community and operational stability in the consumer finance space.
pros: Family-owned business with over 30 years of operating history in Houston
Accepts diverse collateral types including jewelry, tools, electronics, firearms, and musical instruments
Offers gold and jewelry buying service for customers seeking immediate cash
Physical storefront location with walk-in availability in east Houston
Explicitly emphasizes treating customers with respect and dignity
Advertises high-quality merchandise and competitive values on resale items
Wide variety of inventory categories including tools, electronics, musical instruments, and lawn equipment
cons: Website does not disclose specific loan terms, interest rates, or fee structure
No information provided about loan approval process, requirements, or timeframes
Limited online functionality—no online application, quote tool, or detailed service descriptions
No clear disclosure of loan-to-value ratios, maximum loan amounts, or repayment terms
Website lacks customer reviews, testimonials, or ratings to validate claims of being 'high-rated'
best_for: Customers with immediate cash needs who own valuable items to pawn or sell
Individuals seeking to liquidate jewelry, gold, tools, or electronics quickly
Buyers looking for secondhand items at discounted prices from a local retailer
Borrowers seeking short-term collateral-based loans outside traditional banking
diagnosis: Big City Pawn is best for Houston-area customers with immediate cash needs who own valuable items to pawn or those seeking discounted secondhand merchandise. The primary caveat is the complete absence of transparent loan terms, rates, or fees on their website, requiring customers to visit in-person or call to understand exact borrowing costs.
services: Pawn loans on personal items and valuables
Purchase and resale of power tools and hand tools
Purchase and resale of electronics and computers
Purchase and resale of firearms and firearm accessories
Purchase and resale of musical instruments
Purchase and resale of lawn and garden equipment
Purchase and resale of jewelry and watches
In-person customer consultations at physical location
